worksheets.title: Quick check: identity
worksheets.instruction: You can hold more than one role at the same time. Pick three roles from the word bank that could describe one person.

1.instruction: One person can have lots of roles. Tap three words that could all describe the same person.

6.instruction: Multiple identity means holding several roles or communities at once. Select three roles that could belong to one person.

worksheets.submitMessage: Nice. Different roles matter more in different places, and that is multiple identity.

worksheets.title: Quick check: migration
worksheets.instruction: Is each factor a push (makes people leave) or a pull (attracts people to a new country)? Tap the best label for each item.

items.text: War or conflict in someone's home country
Better job opportunities in another country
Poverty and lack of work at home
Safety, stability, and a fresh start

1.instruction: Push means people are pushed away from home. Pull means a new place attracts them. Tap push or pull for each line.

6.instruction: Classify each factor as push (forces people to leave) or pull (draws people to a new country).

worksheets.submitMessage: Well done. Most migration happens because of both push and pull factors together.

worksheets.title: Quick check: belonging
worksheets.instruction: Does each example belong mainly to your local community or to the wider UK? Tap the best fit.

items.text: Your local youth club on your street
Laws made by UK Parliament for the whole country
NHS healthcare available across the UK

1.instruction: Local means your street, borough, or neighbourhood. Wider UK means the whole country. Tap local or UK for each one.

6.instruction: Decide whether each example is mainly about local belonging or belonging to wider UK society.

worksheets.submitMessage: Good thinking. We belong in our local area and as part of the wider UK.
